summary:
Composite resins have hard filler particles that are harder than tooth enamel, making traditional polishing difficult because polishing tools need to be softer than the material they're acting upon. This leads to increased risks of scratching or abrading the restored surface instead of achieving a smooth finish.

The summary above meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focused strictly on clinically significant aspects related to why polishing composites is problematic.
